audience: Students learning remotely at Jumeirah College

hero.title: This is your school day. We are just doing it online.
hero.subtitle: You do not need to do this perfectly. You do need to log in, keep going, and ask for help when you need it.
hero.quickStartNote: You do not need to feel ready. Just begin.

headings.supportTitle: What do you need right now?
headings.supportCaption: Pick one. Do the steps. Come back.
headings.phraseCaption: Simple phrases students can use when they are stuck.
headings.supportPageIntro: If things feel difficult, you are not on your own.

helperCards.title: I miss the start of my lessons
steps: Set an alarm one minute before each lesson starts.
Print or write out your timetable.
Keep your timetable beside your laptop.
Check what lesson is next before finishing the current one.
Add your timetable to Google Calendar with a reminder.
helperCards.note: Use reminders to stay on track.

helperCards.title: I cannot focus
steps: Set a five-minute timer.
Put your phone away.
Sit up and look at the screen.
Close tabs you do not need.
Try the Pomodoro method.

helperCards.title: I feel overwhelmed
steps: Pick one task.
Ignore everything else for now.
Work for five minutes only.
Write down what comes next.

helperCards.title: My eyes hurt
steps: Every 20 minutes, look 20 metres away for 20 seconds.
Lower your screen brightness.
Blink slowly.
Stand up for a moment.
helperCards.note: Then come back and do one small task.

helperCards.title: I do not like reading on a screen
steps: Try listening instead of reading everything yourself.
Turn on VoiceOver on a Mac with Command plus F5.
Turn on Narrator on Windows with Control plus Windows key plus Enter.
Use text-to-speech to follow long instructions.
helperCards.note: Listening can make long tasks feel easier.
extras: Windows shortcut: Control plus Windows key plus Enter

helperCards.title: I need help
steps: Subject teacher for your work
Form tutor for daily support
Head of Year if something is wrong
Counsellor if you feel stressed or overwhelmed
Support for Learning if you need extra structure
helperCards.note: You are not on your own.

helperCards.title: I miss my friends
steps: Message a friend to check in.
Arrange a quick video or voice call.
Join group chats or study calls if you can.
Remember this is temporary.
helperCards.note: Staying connected helps you feel better.

helperCards.title: Wellbeing and support
steps: Reach out to the counselling team if things feel heavy.
Use a short reset routine before the next lesson.
Ask for support early instead of waiting.

dayExpectations: Join every lesson live and on time.
Complete your work and meet deadlines.
Complete your Class Charts wellbeing check-in every day.
lessonRules: Your work will be on Google Classroom.
Your teacher will support you during the lesson.
Teachers may use Google Classroom, Google Meet, email, or chat.
onlineExpectations: Keep your camera on when asked.
Be kind to others and yourself.

supportPhrases.text: I do not understand step one.

supportBullets: Support to get started and keep going
wellbeingHabits: Keep your phone away when you are working.
Take time away from the screen.
wellbeingResetSteps: Focus on one small next step.

wellbeingResources.title: Breathing video
wellbeingResources.description: A short mindfulness video to slow things down before your next step.

wellbeingResources.title: Stretching reset
wellbeingResources.description: A quick physical reset when you have been at your screen for too long.

supportContacts.label: Form tutor
supportContacts.value: Contact your form tutor through the school platform

footer.title: You do not need to be perfect.
footer.text: You just need to log in, start, keep going, and ask for help.
footer.note: Weekly bulletin links and live school updates can be added by editing the content file for this site.
